◀

Unknown organism

Vibrio natriegens — ultra-fast growing bacterium. Fastest-growing non-pathogenic organism known. Potential chassis for synthetic biology & bioproduction.

◀

Initial hypothesis

Varying carbon sources (glucose) and salts (NaCl, MOPS buffer) could optimize growth rate in defined minimal media. AI designed a 2³ full factorial experiment.

Step 3 of 3
The Full Story — ViNatX Pitch
Final Results

Growth Rate Improvement

+16%

LBv2 + 100 mM glutamate vs bare LBv2
μ = 0.688 h⁻¹ vs 0.592 h⁻¹

Optimal Seeding Density

10% v/v

Fastest growth — μ = 0.667 h⁻¹
Doubling time ~62 min

Rounds Completed

3

Round 1: 12-condition media screen
Round 2: supplement optimization
Round 3: glutamate dose-response titration

Key Discovery

Glutamate

Clear dose-response — more glutamate = faster growth. Not yet plateaued at 100 mM. Glucose actively hurts.

Next: push glutamate above 100 mM · 2³ media optimization experiment ready to run
